title: platform done staleness node wired
purpose: D6.4 defines platform done staleness node wired for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse.
scope: Owns `D6.4` only; siblings under `D6` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §6 — Branch D — Platform evolution plane (parallel queue)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#6-branch-d-platform-evolution-plane-parallel-queue).
behavior_steps: Before dequeue, platform done audit wires the promoted artifact as a staleness graph node in program or manifest JSON with upstream dependency edges and pack scope.

[D5.2](D5.2-extend-backwards-compatible-staleness-bump.md) extend promotions bump this graph so reconcile-stale and reconcile-artifact-graph mark downstream design and tasks when the artifact changes.

Together with [D6.1](D6.1-platform-done-catalog-index-row.md)–[D6.3](D6.3-platform-done-task-card-references.md), D6.4 is the final audit gate per [D6-index](D6-index.md) before the queue item marks done.
Missing staleness node means silent consumer drift after platform edit—audit re-enqueues the promotion until node id and edges land on disk.
If the conductor marks promotion done without staleness wiring, D6 audit fails closed at H2 and the head item stays on the platform queue.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Platform queue depth 0 but product blocked on missing playbook â€” D3.3 priority cut skips platform drain.
Edge case `D6.4` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `D6.4`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Scheduler in autopilot workflow: `(steps_total % K) == 0` â†’ platform turn.
playbook-keeper + script extraction skills dequeue promotion items.
Validate `D6.4` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `D6.4` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `D6.4`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: D6.4 defines platform done staleness node wired for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse. In practice, the system before dequeue, platform done audit wires the promoted artifact as a staleness graph node in program or manifest json with upstream dependency edges and pack scope, then [d5.2](d5.2-extend-backwards-compatible-staleness-bump.md) extend promotions bump this graph so reconcile-stale and reconcile-artifact-graph mark downstream design and tasks when the artifact changes.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-d 2026-06-28 -->

Staleness graph wiring completes platform done: promoted artifact registers as a node in program or manifest staleness with upstream dependencies so reconcile-stale and reconcile-artifact-graph mark downstream design and tasks when the artifact changes ([D5.2](D5.2-extend-backwards-compatible-staleness-bump.md) extends bump this graph).

Missing staleness node means silent consumer drift after platform edit. Wire node id, dependency edges, and pack scope in manifest JSON. Together with [D6.1](D6.1-platform-done-catalog-index-row.md)–[D6.3](D6.3-platform-done-task-card-references.md), D6.4 is the audit gate before queue item done—see [D6-index](D6-index.md).
